primary_evidence: T3_current stable=9; S3_equal11 stable=26; S4_equal11 stable=4
allowed_statement: Equal-length controls also lose many pairs; window length may account for a large part of the T3 drop.
forbidden_statement: Do not claim T3 pair loss is only due to missing indices before checking equal-length controls.

primary_evidence: T3_current stable=9; max expanded stable=19; best=T3_expand_backward17_101_117
allowed_statement: Expanding T3 recovers additional stable pairs; best variant is T3_expand_backward17_101_117 with stable=19, gain=10.
forbidden_statement: Do not treat expansion recovery as proof of physical causality; it is a window-sensitivity result.

primary_evidence: base old-old=2, base new-new=6; expanded max old-old=8, expanded max new-new=5
allowed_statement: Expanded windows recover old-index pairs; part of the T3 drop may be due to short/edge placement rather than only index projection mismatch.
forbidden_statement: Do not summarize this audit only as 'T3 is too short' without checking whether recovered pairs are old-index or new-index pairs.
